div.p: Implementation Guidance: For network-intensive servers, io_uring with SQPOLL is the definitive standard. It eliminates syscall overhead and provides predictable tail latency for high-concurrency workloads.

div.h3: Latency CDF, Throughput Scaling, and Syscall Comparison

g.text: I/O Control Plane (100,000 requests, Batch Size=64)

div.p: Observation: io_uring demonstrates O(1) scalability relative to active file descriptors. epoll throughput collapses at 50K due to eventpoll lock contention and O(n) scan overhead during wakeups.
p99 latency is 5.8x lower with io_uring. Context switch overhead is bypassed via shared ring buffers.
IORING_SETUP_SQPOLL offloads submissions to a kernel thread, reducing user-space overhead by 99.9%.
epoll saturates at 120K req/s on this hardware. io_uring scales linearly to 280K req/s.

ul.li: Warm-up: 10s steady state prior to measurement.
CPU Pinning: IRQ affinity set to NUMA node 0.
